en: If your company performs any of the following services and is required to maintain the relevant certification(s) for those services, you will be asked to provide your certificate in a subsequent form.
Canada Contractor License Quebec (RBQ) Applicability Assessment

The Contractor License issued by the Quebec Building Authority (RBQ) governs a company’s right to carry out or have construction work carried out. The Building Act (Chapter B-1.1) requires that any building contractor offering services for carrying out or having a third party carry out construction work in the province of Quebec holds a license in good standing.
Does your company carry out or have construction work carried out for others for profit in Québec and is required to maintain a Contractor License issued by the Quebec Building Authority (RBQ)?
In Québec, any company who carries out construction work, or has construction work carried out, must hold a license, unless they are exempted from doing so in the Building Act or its regulations.
Please select from the following reasons your company is exempt from needing a Contractor License issued by the Quebec Building Authority (RBQ).
The company falls under an exemption outlined in the Building Act or its regulations and is willing to provide evidence of this exemption.
The company does not carry out construction work nor has construction work carried out in Québec.
The company does not operate in Québec.
Please explain why your company is exempt from needing a Contractor License issued by the Quebec Building Authority (RBQ).
